item.title: Opinion | Our Trade With China Has Helped Americans
item.description: Tens of millions of U.S. households gained from lower prices for shoes, clothing and other household necessities.

item.title: U.S. Has a Big Ask for China: Buy Less Oil from Russia, More From America
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent weighs pushing the tricky trade-off along with other economic goals prior to the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: China Signals New Era of Slower Economic Growth
item.description: China set a historically low growth target of 4.5% to 5%, suggesting that the world’s second-largest economy is entering an era of slower expansion.

item.title: China PMIs Send Mixed Signals as Markets Watch for Stimulus
item.description: Activity in China’s sprawling manufacturing sector contracted further last month, in part due to a weeklong holiday.

item.title: China Building Subs That Can Strike U.S. From Closer to Home, U.S. Navy Warns
item.description: Beijing’s accelerating production of submarines is set to challenge U.S. undersea dominance.

item.title: China’s ‘Two Sessions’ to Shed Light on What Lies Ahead for Economy
item.description: At the National People’s Congress, representatives are expected to approve a five-year economic blueprint that will prioritize turning China into a technological superpower.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.

item.title: U.S. Builds Tech Bloc to Counter China, Adds India as Newest Member
item.description: With Pax Silica, the Trump administration is retreating from an adversarial stance it took toward allies and friends last year.

item.title: For the First Time, U.S. Sides With Russia and China in Ukraine Energy Dispute
item.description: Washington opposed a resolution backed by many of its allies over the danger posed by attacks on Kyiv’s nuclear energy.
